LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RECOLOGY INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND MOFIFIED IT AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH "A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS" WITHIN THE MEANING OF CC 3294. IDENTIFYING A DIRECTOR, OFFICER, OR MANAGING AGENT THAT ACTED MALICIOUSLY AND/OR RATIFIED THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT IS A MATTER FOR DISCOVERY. DEFENDANT HAS 21-DAYS TO FILE AN ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

ANTHONY'S MEDICAL CLINIC (TRANSACTION ID # 59528976) FILED BY DEFENDANT RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO (SUED UNDER THE FICTICIOUS NAME OF DOE 1)

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA ON ST. ANTHONY FOUNDATION AND ST. ANTHONY MEDICAL CENTER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. IT IS DENIED AS TO THE DRUG PRESCRIPTION RECORDS IN THE POSSESSION OF ST. ANTHONY'S MEDICAL CLINIC FOR A PERIOD OF FOUR MONTHS PRIOR TO THE DATE OF THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT. IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS IT IS GRANTED. THE SCOPE OF DOCUMENTS SOUGHT ARE OVERBROAD, IRRELEVANT AND DO NOT RELATE DIRECTLY TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED. THEIR PRODUCTION WOULD ALSO VIOLATE THE CROSS-DEFENDANT'S CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT OF PRIVACY. A NEW SUBPOENA SOLELY TO OBTAIN THE PRESCRIPTION RECORDS SHOULD BE PREPARED AND SERVED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena On St. Anthony Foundation And St. Anthony Medical Center
Off Calendar Sep-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan L. Kraft, of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3483, for defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT MAYNOR BARRERA Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena On St. Anthony Foundation And St. Anthony Medical Center is granted in part and denied in part. It is denied as to the drug prescription records in the possession of St. Anthony's Medical Clinic for a period of four months prior to the date of the subject accident. In all other respects it is granted. The scope of documents sought are overbroad, irrelevant and do not relate directly to the cause of action alleged. Their production would also violate the Cross-defendant's constitutional right of privacy. A new subpoena solely to obtain the prescription records should be prepared and served. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Discovery Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendant Recology San Francisco Subpoenas Of Documents For Medical Records
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Timothy G. Tietjen of Rouda Feder Tietjen and McGuinn (415-398-5398) appears as counsel for plaijntiff/moving party

Attorney, Andrew Foster Shi of Manning & Kass Ellrod Ramirez Trester LLP (415-217-6990) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Recology San Francisco

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification .

PLAINTIFF JOSEPH PLAMONDON'S Motion To Quash Defendant Recology San Francisco Subpoenas Of Documents For Medical Records is GRANTED in part. The court adopts the proposed ruling and rationale of Judge Pro Tem Elizabeth Riles that the subpoenas should be modified to allow only for the production of medical records from 2006 to the present relating to plaintiff's right lower extremity and to any qualatative and quantative toxicology results in that time period. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Discovery Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendant Recology San Francisco Subpoenas Of Documents For Medical Records

Andrew Festershi, of Manning Kass, Tel. 415.217.6990, for defendants;
Court Reporter: Joanne M. Farrell, CSR No. 4838, Tel. 415.274.9977.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Defendant's counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH PLAMONDON Motion To Quash Defendant Recology San Francisco Subpoenas Of Documents For Medical Records is continued to September 14, 2016, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Defendant is informed that a $30. Court Reporter fee will be assessed and must be paid before the hearing date. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth Riles; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Joanne M. Farrell, CSR No. 4838, Tel. 415.274.9977; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mandissa Logan of Rouda, Feder, Tietjen & McGuinn telephone number 415-398-5398 for Plaintiff.
Mark Hagopian of Manning & Kass telephone number 415-297-6990 for Defendant.

The matter was discussed. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling and mofified it as follows: DEFENDANT RECOLOGY INC'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts showing that defendant acted with "a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others" within the meaning of CC 3294. Identifying a director, officer, or managing agent that acted maliciously and/or ratified the alleged misconduct is a matter for discovery. Defendant has 21-days to file an answer. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)
